response: {"calibration_score":0.46,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":10,"biases_detected":["anchoring — units 20 and 27 assumed specific port/path state (3006, 5006, .env.local) before verifying, leading to cascading bash failures","sunk_cost — unit 8 continued iterating on the heredoc jq approach through three Write/Bash failure cycles rather than switching to a simpler inline strategy after the first failure","availability — unit 24 hardcoded a specific task output path derived from memory of a prior run rather than discovering it dynamically, producing an immediate failure","environmental_optimism — units 20 and 27 assumed dev servers and pm2 processes were running without a pre-check, causing multiple no-op curl/lsof rounds"],"recommendations":["Add an upfront environment probe step (pm2 list, lsof port check, .env existence) before any unit that depends on running services — this pattern caused repeated 3-tool failure sequences in units 20 and 27","When a shell heredoc or file-write approach fails once, switch to the Write tool on the second attempt rather than retrying Bash; the unit 8 failure-recovery cycle took 3 extra tool calls","For task output file paths, derive the path dynamically (glob or ls) rather than constructing it from session memory — the hardcoded path in unit 24 is brittle across sessions","The session's overall calibration is sound (0.46) with no underconfidence detected; the primary drag is early-unit environmental overconfidence that self-corrects quickly — a one-time env-probe at session start would raise the score to ~0.6"]}
